pi_items.source: setup_task.sh:23, export_result.sh:12 - directory created and configured by task setup script
setup_task.sh:79 (docker-compose.yml port mapping '8090:80'), export_result.sh:86 (curl test on localhost:8090)
setup_task.sh:86-90 explicitly documents 4 violations: (1) no USER directive, (2) docker.sock mount, (3) privileged mode, (4) no resource limits. Verifier.py checks all 4 items.
setup_task.sh:95, export_result.sh:26,96 - image tag for hardened container build target

privileged_info_summary: The task is a container security hardening challenge with a synthetic nginx application at /home/ga/insecure-app running on port 8090. The application is intentionally configured with exactly 4 security violations: (1) running as root (no USER directive in Dockerfile), (2) mounting the Docker socket at /var/run/docker.sock, (3) using privileged:true mode in docker-compose.yml, and (4) lacking resource limits (memory and CPU constraints). Agents must identify and fix all violations by modifying the Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml, then rebuild and redeploy the hardened container image as secure-web:hardened.
